promote.Description: Adopted and reconciled. I come as a child in good standing, holy, blameless, without fault.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For you did not receive a spirit of slavery that returns you to fear, but you received the Spirit of sonship, by whom we cry, "Abba! Father!"

promote.Description: Origin and creator. Before time, after time, on time.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "I am the Alpha and the Omega," says the Lord God, "who is and who was and who is to come, the Almighty."

promote.Description: Nothing is too big for You. Faith the size of a mustard seed moves mountains.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "Because of your little faith," He told them. "For truly I tell you, if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, 'Move from here to there,' and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you."

promote.Description: Nothing precedes You. You are before all things.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End."

promote.Description: Not created. You have no origin because You are the origin.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Before the mountains were born or You brought forth the earth and the world, from everlasting to everlasting You are God.

promote.Description: Resurrection is easy for You.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): He said, "If you will listen carefully to the voice of the LORD your God, and do what is right in His eyes, and pay attention to His commandments, and keep all His statutes, I will not bring on you any of the diseases I brought on the Egyptians. For I am the LORD who heals you."

promote.Description: All will stand and bow. Every account will be settled.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The sea gave up its dead, and Death and Hades gave up their dead, and each one was judged according to his deeds.

promote.Description: Vengeance belongs to You. You will repay.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For we know Him who said, "Vengeance is Mine; I will repay," and again, "The Lord will judge His people."

promote.Description: You dwell in light no one can approach. You are beyond comprehension.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): He alone is immortal and dwells in unapproachable light. No one has ever seen Him, nor can anyone see Him. To Him be honor and eternal dominion! Amen.

promote.Description: Let me stay connected to You. Apart from You I can do nothing.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "I am the vine; you are the branches. The one who remains in Me, and I in him, will bear much fruit. For apart from Me you can do nothing."

promote.Description: Stay on track. You are the only path to the Father.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Jesus answered, "I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me."

promote.Description: Everything else is a lie. You are the standard against which all reality is measured.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Jesus answered, "I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me."

promote.Description: What I see fades. Even in my dying, He is life.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Jesus answered, "I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through Me."

promote.Description: You are patient, kind, calm, enduring. Your love defines love itself.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Whoever does not love does not know God, because God is love.

promote.Description: No rush to be done. Your patience leads to repentance.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Or do you disregard the riches of His kindness, tolerance, and patience, not realizing that God's kindness leads you to repentance?

promote.Description: Playing infinite chess. You search the heart and test the mind.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I, the LORD, search the heart; I examine the mind to reward a man according to his way, by what his deeds deserve.

promote.Description: Before time, after time, on time, and willing to go overtime.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Before the mountains were born or You brought forth the earth and the world, from everlasting to everlasting You are God.

promote.Description: Everything You do is right. Your character is the definition of justice.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The LORD is righteous in all His ways and loving in all His deeds.

promote.Description: Reality would shatter without You. You sustain every particle.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): He is before all things, and in Him all things hold together.

promote.Description: Immovable, beyond erosion. You do not change.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "For I, the LORD, do not change; therefore you, O sons of Jacob, have not been consumed."

promote.Description: Incomparable. Who has measured the waters in the hollow of His hand?
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "To whom will you compare Me, or who is My equal?" asks the Holy One.

promote.Description: Every good gift comes from You. None is from my strength.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Every good and perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of the heavenly lights, who does not change like shifting shadows.

promote.Description: You alone can track infinite particles at a quantum level.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): He determines the number of the stars; He calls them each by name.

promote.Description: Your breath created all that is and holds it together at infinite scale.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): By the word of the LORD the heavens were made, and all the stars by the breath of His mouth.

promote.Description: No one can withstand Your power. You rule over all.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): and said, "O LORD, God of our fathers, are You not the God who is in heaven, and do You not rule over all the kingdoms of the nations? Power and might are in Your hand, and no one can stand against You."

promote.Description: You do not faint or grow weary. Your understanding is unsearchable.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Do you not know? Have you not heard? The LORD is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He will not grow tired or weary; His understanding is beyond searching out.

promote.Description: You do not cast away forever. You devise means for the banished to return.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For surely we will die and be like water spilled on the ground, which cannot be recovered. Yet God does not take away a life; instead, He devises ways so that the banished one may not remain estranged from Him.

promote.Description: Sufficient. You are not served by human hands as if You needed anything.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The God who made the world and everything in it is the Lord of heaven and earth and does not live in temples made by human hands. Nor is He served by human hands, as if He needed anything, because He Himself gives everyone life and breath and everything else.

promote.Description: Gracious and compassionate. Full of mercy.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The LORD is gracious and righteous; our God is compassionate.

promote.Description: You revive courage. You are near the brokenhearted.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): But You, O LORD, are a shield around me, my glory, and the lifter of my head.

promote.Description: Present tense. You are not past tense or future tense. You are here, now.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): God said to Moses, "I AM WHO I AM. This is what you are to say to the Israelites: 'I AM has sent me to you.'"

promote.Description: You inhabit eternity. Yet You are with the contrite and lowly.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For thus says the One who is high and lifted up, who inhabits eternity, whose name is Holy: "I dwell in the high and holy place, and also with the contrite and lowly in spirit, to revive the spirit of the lowly and to revive the heart of the contrite."

promote.Description: God is Spirit. Not confined to matter, space, or form.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): God is Spirit, and His worshipers must worship Him in spirit and in truth.

promote.Description: You are not a distant force. You know me by name and count the hairs on my head.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered.

promote.Description: Able conductor. All things are under Your authority.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): All the peoples of the earth are counted as nothing, and He does as He pleases with the army of heaven and the peoples of the earth. There is no one who can restrain His hand or say to Him, 'What have You done?'

promote.Description: You provided the sacrifice. The debt has been paid.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): God presented Him as the atoning sacrifice through faith in His blood, in order to demonstrate His righteousness, because in His forbearance He had passed over the sins committed beforehand.

promote.Description: You are the last word. History concludes in You.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.

promote.Description: El Shaddai. God All-Sufficient, God Almighty.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the LORD appeared to him and said, "I am God Almighty. Walk before Me and be blameless."

promote.Description: The armies of heaven are at Your command.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "Neither," He replied. "I have now come as Commander of the LORD's army." Then Joshua fell facedown in reverence and asked Him, "What does my Lord have to say to His servant?"

promote.Description: You are my exceeding joy. My gladness has a name.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Then I will go to the altar of God, to God my exceeding joy. I will praise You with the harp, O God, my God.

promote.Description: You are not far off. You chose to dwell among us.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "Behold, the virgin will be with child and will give birth to a son, and they will call Him Immanuel" — which means, "God with us."

promote.Description: My source, strength, wisdom, guide, purpose. Christ in me, the hope of glory.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): To them God has chosen to make known among the Gentiles the glorious riches of this mystery, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.

promote.Description: All I need. You supply according to Your riches in glory.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): And my God will supply all your needs according to His glorious riches in Christ Jesus.

promote.Description: You protect and lead. I shall not want.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.

promote.Name / Attribute: He Was, Is, and Is to Come
promote.Description: Not hidden in time or by time. You transcend every moment.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): And each of the four living creatures had six wings and was covered with eyes all around and within. Day and night they never stop saying: "Holy, Holy, Holy, is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is to come!"

promote.Description: Relentless, chasing, seeking, finding, returning, restoring.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and loses one of them, does not leave the ninety-nine in the open pasture and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?"

promote.Description: In the beginning was the Word. You spoke and creation obeyed.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

promote.Description: Not an idol of stone. You are alive, active, and present.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For to this end we toil and strive, because we have set our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all men, and especially of those who believe.

promote.Description: Your holiness burns away everything impure.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For our God is a consuming fire.

promote.Description: You buy back what was lost. You reclaim what the enemy stole.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): This is what the LORD, the King of Israel and his Redeemer, the LORD of Hosts, says: "I am the first and I am the last, and there is no God but Me."

promote.Description: Sovereign ruler. King of kings, Lord of lords.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): which God will bring about in His own time — He who is blessed and the only Sovereign, the King of kings and Lord of lords.

promote.Description: Your wisdom is unsearchable. Your worth is beyond measure.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Who has measured the waters in the hollow of His hand, or marked off the heavens with the span of His hand? Who has held the dust of the earth in a basket, or weighed the mountains on a scale and the hills with a balance?

promote.Description: You are the source. Life itself originates in You.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): You killed the Author of life, but God raised Him from the dead, and we are witnesses of the fact.

promote.Description: You are not predictable. You work beyond my imagination.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Beloved, do not be surprised at the fiery trial that has come upon you, as though something strange were happening to you.

promote.Description: You need nothing from anyone. You are wholly self-sufficient.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The God who made the world and everything in it is the Lord of heaven and earth and does not live in temples made by human hands. Nor is He served by human hands, as if He needed anything, because He Himself gives everyone life and breath and everything else.

promote.Description: From His glorious, unlimited resources He empowers with inner strength.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I pray that out of the riches of His glory, He may strengthen you with power through His Spirit in your inner being.

promote.Description: I delight in the Lord. He delights in me.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Delight yourself in the LORD, and He will give you the desires of your heart.

promote.Description: There is a record of each one. Nothing is forgotten.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): You have taken account of my wanderings; put my tears in Your bottle. Are they not in Your book?

promote.Name / Attribute: He Rewards Those Who Seek Him
promote.Description: Drawing near is never wasted. He is found by those who look.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): And without faith it is impossible to please God, because anyone who comes to Him must believe that He exists and that He rewards those who earnestly seek Him.

promote.Description: He hears me. He is for me.
promote.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Then my enemies will turn back on the day I call. This I know: God is for me.

worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): God said to Moses, 'I AM WHO I AM.'
God spoke to Moses: 'I am the LORD. I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as God Almighty.'
But You, O LORD, sit enthroned forever; Your renown endures through all generations.
I am the LORD; that is My name! I will not give My glory to another.
For I, the LORD, do not change; therefore you, O sons of Jacob, have not been consumed.
Truly, truly, I tell you, before Abraham was born, I am!
Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Then God said, 'Let Us make man in Our image, after Our likeness.'
The heavens declare the glory of God; the skies proclaim the work of His hands.
The LORD is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He will not grow tired or weary.
Ah, Lord GOD! You have made the heavens and earth by Your great power. Nothing is too difficult for You!
You alone are the LORD. You made the heavens and all their host, the earth and all that is on it.
Through Him all things were made, and without Him nothing was made that has been made.
Abram said, 'O Lord GOD, what can You give me, since I remain childless?'
For the LORD your God is God of gods and Lord of lords, the great, mighty, and awesome God.
The mountains melt like wax at the presence of the LORD, at the presence of the Lord of all the earth.
I saw the Lord seated on a high and lofty throne, and the train of His robe filled the temple.
'Ah, Lord GOD,' I said, 'I do not know how to speak, for I am only a youth.'
A son honors his father, and a servant his master. If I am a father, where is My honor? If I am a master, where is My fear?
Sovereign Lord, as You have promised, You now dismiss Your servant in peace.
I am God Almighty. Walk before Me and be blameless.
I am God Almighty. Be fruitful and multiply.
I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as God Almighty.
He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High will rest in the shadow of the Almighty.
Like the voice of the Almighty, like the tumult of an army.
For the day of the LORD is near; it will come as destruction from the Almighty.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, says the Lord God, who is and was and is to come, the Almighty.
Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
The oracle of one who hears the words of God, who has knowledge from the Most High.
I will give thanks to the LORD for His righteousness and sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.
For You, O LORD, are Most High over all the earth; You are exalted far above all gods.
I praised the Most High and honored and glorified Him who lives forever. His dominion is everlasting.
He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High.
However, the Most High does not dwell in houses made by human hands.
Abraham named that place 'The LORD Will Provide.' So to this day it is said, 'On the mountain of the LORD it will be provided.'
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
And my God will supply all your needs according to His glorious riches in Christ Jesus.
Look at the birds of the air: They do not sow or reap or gather into barns — and yet your heavenly Father feeds them.
Consider the ravens: They do not sow or reap; they have no storehouse or barn — yet God feeds them.
And God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that in all things, at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.
Let us then approach the throne of grace with confidence, so that we may receive mercy and find grace to help us in our time of need.
For I am the LORD who heals you.
He forgives all your iniquities and heals all your diseases.
By His stripes we are healed.
Heal me, O LORD, and I will be healed; save me, and I will be saved, for You are my praise.
He drove out the spirits with a word and healed all the sick, to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah.
The Spirit of the Lord is on Me, because He has anointed Me to preach good news to the poor. He has sent Me to proclaim liberty to the captives.
The prayer offered in faith will restore the one who is sick. Therefore confess your sins to one another and pray for one another, so that you may be healed.
Moses built an altar and called it 'The LORD Is My Banner.'
We will shout for joy at your victory and raise a banner in the name of our God.
You have given a banner to those who fear You, that it may be displayed because of the truth.
In that day the Root of Jesse will stand as a banner for the peoples. The nations will seek Him, and His place of rest will be glorious.
Raise a signal flag toward Zion! Seek refuge, do not delay!
In all these things we are more than conquerors through Him who loved us.
But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!
Gideon built an altar to the LORD there and called it 'The LORD Is Peace.'
And He will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.
For He Himself is our peace, who has made the two one and has torn down the dividing wall of hostility.
Let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, since as members of one body you were called to peace.
And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
Peace I leave with you; My peace I give to you. I do not give as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled; do not be afraid.
Therefore, since we have been justified by faith, we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
He tends His flock like a shepherd; He gathers the lambs in His arms and carries them close to His heart.
For this is what the Lord GOD says: I Myself will search for My sheep and look for them.
I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep.
I am the good shepherd. I know My sheep, and My sheep know Me.
Now may the God of peace, who brought back from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great Shepherd of the sheep.
For you were like sheep going astray, but now you have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.
He will be called: The LORD Our Righteousness.
And this is the name by which she will be called: The LORD Our Righteousness.
But now, apart from the law, the righteousness of God has been revealed, through faith in Jesus Christ to all who believe.
Christ Jesus, who became to us wisdom from God, and righteousness and sanctification and redemption.
God made Him who knew no sin to be sin on our behalf, so that in Him we might become the righteousness of God.
Not having a righteousness of my own from the law, but that which comes through faith in Christ.
For Christ is the end of the law, bringing righteousness to everyone who believes.
And the name of the city from that day on will be: THE LORD IS THERE.
God is within her; she will not be moved. God will help her at the break of day.
And surely I am with you always, even to the end of the age.
Now the dwelling of God is with man, and He will dwell with them. They will be His people, and God Himself will be with them.
The LORD your God is among you, a mighty one who saves. He will rejoice over you with gladness; He will quiet you with His love.
I will ask the Father, and He will give you another Advocate to be with you forever — the Spirit of truth.
For we are the temple of the living God. As God has said: 'I will dwell among them and walk among them.'
I am the Alpha and the Omega, who is and was and is to come, the Almighty.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, the Beginning and the End. To the thirsty I will give freely from the spring of the water of life.
I am the Alpha and the Omega, the First and the Last, the Beginning and the End.
Who has performed and accomplished it, calling forth the generations from the beginning? I, the LORD — the first and with the last — I am He.
I am the first and I am the last, and there is no God but Me.
Listen to Me, O Jacob, and Israel, whom I have called: I am He; I am the first, and I am the last.
He is before all things, and in Him all things hold together.

worship365.Name (English): Lion of the Tribe of Judah
worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): See, the Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, has triumphed.

worship365.Name (English): Lion of the Tribe of Judah
worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): Judah is a young lion. The scepter will not depart from Judah until the coming of the One to whom it belongs.

worship365.Name (English): Lion of the Tribe of Judah
worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): They will follow the LORD; He will roar like a lion. When He roars, His children will come trembling from the west.

worship365.Name (English): Lion of the Tribe of Judah
worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): The lion has roared — who will not fear? The Lord GOD has spoken — who will not prophesy?

worship365.Name (English): Lion of the Tribe of Judah
worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): The wicked flee when no one pursues, but the righteous are as bold as a lion.

worship365.Name (English): Lion of the Tribe of Judah
worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): For it is clear that our Lord descended from Judah.

worship365.Name (English): Lion of the Tribe of Judah
worship365.Key Phrase (BSB): He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High. He will reign over the house of Jacob forever.
Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
When he saw Jesus passing by, he said, 'Look, the Lamb of God!'
Then I saw a Lamb, looking as if it had been slain, standing in the center of the throne.
For the Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to springs of living water.
You were redeemed not with perishable things like silver or gold, but with the precious blood of Christ, a lamb without blemish or defect.
He was oppressed and afflicted, yet He did not open His mouth. He was led like a lamb to the slaughter.
Like a sheep He was led to the slaughter. Then Philip began with that very Scripture and told him the good news about Jesus.
They have forsaken the LORD; they have despised the Holy One of Israel.
But the LORD of Hosts will be exalted by His justice, and the Holy God will show Himself holy by His righteousness.
For I am the LORD your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior.
I will praise You with the harp for Your faithfulness, O my God; I will sing praises to You with the lyre, O Holy One of Israel.
For our shield belongs to the LORD, our king to the Holy One of Israel.
For she has acted proudly against the LORD, against the Holy One of Israel.
For I am God and not man, the Holy One among you.
Thrones were set in place, and the Ancient of Days took His seat. His clothing was white as snow, and the hair of His head like pure wool.
One like a son of man was coming with the clouds of heaven. He approached the Ancient of Days and was led into His presence.
Until the Ancient of Days came and pronounced judgment in favor of the saints of the Most High.
From everlasting to everlasting, You are God.
Your years go on through all generations. They will perish, but You will remain; they will all wear out like a garment. But You remain the same, and Your years will never end.
Even from eternity I am He, and no one can deliver out of My hand. When I act, who can reverse it?
Holy, Holy, Holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is to come!
Will not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?
God is a righteous judge, and a God who feels indignation every day.
And the heavens proclaim His righteousness, for God Himself is judge.
For the LORD is our judge, the LORD is our lawgiver, the LORD is our king; He will save us.
He has set a day when He will judge the world with justice by the man He has appointed.
To God the judge of all men, to the spirits of the righteous made perfect.
Then I saw a great white throne and Him who sat on it. And the dead were judged according to their deeds.
O righteous God who searches hearts and minds.
The LORD is righteous in all His ways and loving in all His deeds.
There is no God but Me — a righteous God and Savior.
Righteous are You, O LORD, when I bring a case before You.
Righteous Father, though the world does not know You, I know You.
He did this to demonstrate His righteousness, so that He would be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
If anyone does sin, we have an advocate before the Father — Jesus Christ, the Righteous One.
He is the Rock, His works are perfect, and all His ways are just. A faithful God who does no wrong, upright and just is He.
The ordinances of the LORD are sure and altogether righteous.
Righteous are You, O LORD, and Your judgments are just.
He did this to be just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus.
What then shall we say? Is God unjust? Certainly not!
Yes, Lord God Almighty, true and just are Your judgments.
After all, it is only right for God to repay with affliction those who afflict you.
Be holy, because I am holy.
Holy, Holy, Holy is the LORD of Hosts; the whole earth is full of His glory!
There is no one holy like the LORD; there is no one besides You; there is no Rock like our God.
Holy, Holy, Holy is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is to come!
Who will not fear You, Lord, and glorify Your name? For You alone are holy.
But just as He who called you is holy, so be holy in all you do. For it is written: Be holy, because I am holy.
God disciplines us for our good, so that we may share in His holiness.
O LORD, You have searched me and known me. You know when I sit and when I rise; You perceive my thoughts from afar.
For the LORD is a God who knows, and by Him actions are weighed.
Do you understand the balancing of the clouds, the wondrous works of Him who is perfect in knowledge?
The eyes of the LORD are in every place, observing the evil and the good.
Who has directed the Spirit of the LORD, or who has counseled Him and instructed Him?
Nothing in all creation is hidden from God's sight. Everything is uncovered and exposed before the eyes of Him to whom we must give account.
Even if our hearts condemn us, God is greater than our hearts, and He knows all things.
Where can I go from Your Spirit? Where can I flee from Your presence? If I ascend to the heavens, You are there; if I make my bed in Sheol, You are there.
Am I only a God nearby, and not a God far away? Do I not fill heaven and earth?
He is not far from each one of us. For in Him we live and move and have our being.
Heaven is My throne and the earth is My footstool. What kind of house will you build for Me?
The eyes of the LORD are in every place, observing the evil and the good.
For where two or three gather in My name, there am I with them.
One God and Father of all, who is over all and through all and in all.
Is anything too difficult for the LORD?
Nothing is too difficult for You!
With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.
For nothing will be impossible with God.
Hallelujah! For the Lord our God, the Almighty, reigns!
Our God is in the heavens; He does whatever He pleases.
I know that You can do all things and that no plan of Yours can be thwarted.
I, the LORD, do not change.
They will perish, but You will remain. You remain the same, and Your years will never end.
They will be changed, but You are the same, and Your years will never end.
Jesus Christ is the same yesterday and today and forever.
Every good and perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, who does not change like shifting shadows.
The plans of the LORD stand firm forever, the purposes of His heart through all generations.
The grass withers and the flowers fall, but the word of our God stands forever.
The LORD has established His throne in heaven, and His kingdom rules over all.
He does as He pleases with the army of heaven and the peoples of the earth. No one can restrain His hand.
I declare the end from the beginning. My purpose will stand, and I will accomplish all My good pleasure.
In Him we have been chosen, having been predestined according to the plan of Him who works out everything according to the counsel of His will.
I will have mercy on whom I have mercy. It does not depend on man's desire or effort, but on God's mercy.
Who has spoken and it came to pass, unless the Lord has ordained it? Do not both adversity and good come from the mouth of the Most High?
The king's heart is a stream of water in the hand of the LORD; He directs it wherever He pleases.
The LORD, the LORD, a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion and faithfulness.
The LORD is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion.
The LORD is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and great in loving devotion.
But You are a forgiving God, gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion.
Return to the LORD your God, for He is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion.
I knew that You are a gracious and compassionate God, slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion.
The God of all grace, who called you to His eternal glory in Christ, will Himself restore you, establish you, strengthen you, and settle you.
But You, O Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God, slow to anger, abounding in loving devotion and faithfulness.
The LORD is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion.
Because of the LORD's loving devotion we are not consumed, for His mercies never fail. They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness!
Be merciful, just as your Father is merciful.
I will have mercy on whom I have mercy, and I will have compassion on whom I have compassion.
But because of His great love for us, God, who is rich in mercy, made us alive with Christ, even when we were dead in our trespasses.
He saved us, not by the righteous deeds we had done, but according to His mercy.
God is love.
God is love, and the one who abides in love abides in God, and God abides in him.
For God so loved the world that He gave His one and only Son, that everyone who believes in Him shall not perish but have eternal life.
But God demonstrates His own love for us in this: While we were still sinners, Christ died for us.
But because of His great love for us, God, who is rich in mercy.
The LORD did not set His affection on you because you were more numerous. It is because the LORD loved you.
He will rejoice over you with gladness; He will quiet you with His love; He will exult over you with singing.
Know therefore that the LORD your God is God, the faithful God who keeps His covenant of loving devotion for a thousand generations.
O LORD God of Hosts, who is like You? You are mighty, O LORD, and Your faithfulness surrounds You.
Your loving devotion, O LORD, reaches to the heavens, Your faithfulness to the clouds.
They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness!
God is faithful, who has called you into fellowship with His Son, Jesus Christ our Lord.
If we are faithless, He remains faithful, for He cannot deny Himself.
Let us hold resolutely to the hope we profess, for He who promised is faithful.
The LORD is near to all who call on Him, to all who call on Him in truth.
I am the way and the truth and the life.
Now this is eternal life, that they may know You, the only true God.
Let God be found true, and every man a liar.
These are the words of the One who is holy and true.
Great and wonderful are Your works, Lord God Almighty! Just and true are Your ways, O King of the nations!
We know also that the Son of God has come and has given us understanding, so that we may know Him who is true.
The LORD, a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger.
The LORD is slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion, forgiving iniquity and transgression.
The LORD is compassionate and gracious, slow to anger.
God's kindness leads you to repentance.
What if God, choosing to show His wrath and make His power known, bore with great patience the objects of His wrath?
The Lord is not slow in keeping His promise; He is patient with you, not wanting anyone to perish, but everyone to come to repentance.
But You are a forgiving God, gracious and compassionate, slow to anger.
The LORD Will Provide.
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
Look at the birds. Your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?
My God will supply all your needs according to His glorious riches in Christ Jesus.
Consider the ravens: God feeds them. How much more valuable are you than birds!
God is able to make all grace abound to you, so that in all things you will abound in every good work.
Those who seek the LORD will not lack any good thing.
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer.
You are my help and my deliverer; O my God, do not delay.
For I am the LORD your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior.
There is no God but Me — a righteous God and Savior; there is none but Me.
Today in the city of David a Savior has been born to you. He is Christ the Lord!
From this man's descendants, God has brought to Israel the Savior Jesus, as He promised.
We have set our hope on the living God, who is the Savior of all men, and especially of those who believe.
I know that my Redeemer lives, and that in the end He will stand upon the earth.
They remembered that God was their Rock, and the Most High God their Redeemer.
Do not be afraid, O worm Jacob. I Myself will help you, declares the LORD, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.
This is what the LORD says, your Redeemer, who formed you in the womb: I am the LORD, who has made all things.
Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us.
He gave Himself for us to redeem us from all lawlessness and to purify for Himself a people for His own possession.
You were redeemed, not with perishable things like silver or gold, but with the precious blood of Christ.
In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
By the word of the LORD the heavens were made. He spoke, and it came to be.
The LORD is the Creator of the ends of the earth. He will not grow tired or weary.
You alone are the LORD. You made the heavens and all their host.
Through Him all things were made; without Him nothing was made.
For in Him all things were created, things in heaven and on earth, visible and invisible.
You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power, for You created all things.
The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
He tends His flock like a shepherd; He gathers the lambs in His arms.
I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep.
I am the good shepherd; I know My sheep and My sheep know Me.
The God of peace, who brought back from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great Shepherd of the sheep.
You have returned to the Shepherd and Overseer of your souls.
I Myself will search for My sheep and look after them.
God is the King of all the earth; sing to Him a psalm of praise.
The LORD reigns, let the nations tremble; He sits enthroned between the cherubim, let the earth shake.
My eyes have seen the King, the LORD of Hosts!
The LORD is the true God; He is the living God and everlasting King.
On His robe and on His thigh He has this name written: KING OF KINGS AND LORD OF LORDS.
Now to the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever.
Who is this King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty, the LORD mighty in battle.
He is the Rock, His works are perfect, and all His ways are just.
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer; my God is my rock, in whom I take refuge.
My salvation and my honor rest on God; my mighty rock, my refuge is God.
Trust in the LORD forever, for the LORD GOD is an everlasting Rock.
They drank from the spiritual Rock that accompanied them, and that Rock was Christ.
On this rock I will build My church, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it.
But the LORD has been my stronghold, and my God the rock of my refuge.
The LORD is a refuge for the oppressed, a stronghold in times of trouble.
God is our refuge and strength, an ever-present help in times of trouble.
Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts before Him. God is our refuge.
The LORD is good, a stronghold in the day of trouble; He knows those who take refuge in Him.
The eternal God is your refuge, and underneath are the everlasting arms.
I cry to You, O LORD; I say, 'You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.'
O LORD, my strength and my fortress, my refuge in the day of distress.
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer.
I will say of the LORD, 'He is my refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.'
My loving God, my fortress, my stronghold and my deliverer, my shield, in whom I take refuge.
O LORD, my strength and my fortress, my refuge in the day of distress.
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, and my deliverer.
For You are my rock and my fortress; lead me and guide me for the sake of Your name.
The name of the LORD is a strong tower; the righteous run to it and are safe.
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, my deliverer, my God, my stronghold.
The LORD is good, a stronghold in the day of trouble.
O LORD, my strength and my fortress, my refuge in the day of distress.
God is my strong fortress, and He makes my way perfect.
The LORD is my light and my salvation — whom shall I fear? The LORD is the stronghold of my life — whom shall I dread?
For You have been a refuge for the poor, a refuge for the needy in distress, a shelter from the storm.
But I am like a flourishing olive tree in the house of God; I trust in God's loving devotion forever and ever.
The LORD is a warrior; the LORD is His name.
The LORD will march out like a mighty one; He will stir up His zeal like a warrior.
The LORD your God, who goes before you, will fight for you Himself.
I have now come as Commander of the LORD's army.
Who is this King of glory? The LORD strong and mighty, the LORD mighty in battle.
He put on righteousness as His breastplate, and the helmet of salvation on His head.
The LORD your God is among you, a mighty one who saves.
Vengeance is Mine; I will repay. In due time their foot will slip.
Do not avenge yourselves. Vengeance is Mine; I will repay, says the Lord.
The LORD is a jealous and avenging God; the LORD takes vengeance. The LORD is full of wrath.
O LORD, God of vengeance, O God of vengeance, shine forth!
He put on garments of vengeance and wrapped Himself in a cloak of zeal.
I will plead your cause and take vengeance on your behalf.
The Lord is an avenger in all these things, as we told you before and solemnly warned you.
The fire of the LORD burned among them and consumed some of the outskirts of the camp.
The LORD will never be willing to forgive him. His anger and jealousy will burn against that man.
God is a righteous judge, and a God who feels indignation every day.
The LORD is a jealous and avenging God. The LORD takes vengeance and is full of wrath.
The wrath of God is being revealed from heaven against all the godlessness and wickedness of men.
Let no one deceive you with empty words, for because of such things God's wrath comes on the sons of disobedience.
He treads the winepress of the fury of the wrath of God Almighty.
For the LORD your God is a consuming fire, a jealous God.
Know today that the LORD your God goes across ahead of you as a consuming fire. He will destroy them.
For our God is a consuming fire.
The appearance of the LORD's glory on the mountaintop was like a consuming fire.
The name of the LORD comes from afar, with burning anger and dense clouds of smoke.
Lest My wrath break out like fire and burn with no one to quench it.
So I have consumed them with the fire of My fury, bringing their ways down on their own heads.
For I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God.
For the LORD, whose name is Jealous, is a jealous God.
For the LORD your God, who is among you, is a jealous God.
He is a holy God; He is a jealous God. He will not forgive your transgressions or your sins.
Now I will restore Jacob from captivity and have compassion on the whole house of Israel, and I will be jealous for My holy name.
Then the LORD will be jealous for His land and will have compassion on His people.
I am very jealous for Jerusalem and Zion.
He alone is immortal and dwells in unapproachable light. No one has ever seen Him, nor can anyone see Him.
He wraps Himself in light as with a garment; He stretches out the heavens like a tent.
The Light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
In Your light we see light.
The LORD will be your everlasting light, and your God will be your glory.
The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gives it light.
Every good and perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of the heavenly lights.
But there is a God in heaven who reveals mysteries.
The revelation of the mystery hidden for long ages past, but now revealed and made known.
God has revealed it to us by His Spirit. The Spirit searches all things, even the deep things of God.
The mystery made known to me by revelation, which was not made known to men in other generations.
The mystery that has been kept hidden for ages but is now disclosed: Christ in you, the hope of glory.
Surely the Lord GOD does nothing without revealing His plan to His servants the prophets.
The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of God has been given to you.
He restores my soul. He leads me in paths of righteousness for His name's sake.
You will be called Repairer of Broken Walls, Restorer of Streets with Dwellings.
I will restore your health and heal your wounds, declares the LORD.
I will restore to you the years that the swarming locust has eaten.
Heaven must receive Him until the time comes for God to restore everything.
Restore to me the joy of Your salvation, and sustain me with a willing spirit.
Return to your stronghold, O prisoners of hope; even today I declare that I will restore double to you.
Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for You are with me.
I, yes I, am He who comforts you.
The Father of compassion and the God of all comfort, who comforts us in all our troubles.
I will ask the Father, and He will give you another Advocate to be with you forever.
It is for your benefit that I am going away. Unless I go away, the Advocate will not come to you.
May the God of endurance and encouragement grant you harmony with one another.
If you have any encouragement in Christ, any comfort from His love, any fellowship with the Spirit.
The heavens declare the glory of God; the skies proclaim the work of His hands.
Ascribe to the LORD the glory due His name; worship the LORD in the splendor of His holiness.
Holy, Holy, Holy is the LORD of Hosts; the whole earth is full of His glory!
The glory of the immortal God.
The Son is the radiance of God's glory and the exact representation of His nature.
You are worthy, our Lord and God, to receive glory and honor and power.
They will sing of the ways of the LORD, for great is the glory of the LORD.

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my time, my calendar, my schedule.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): My times are in Your hands; deliver me from my enemies and from those who pursue me.

release.Prompt Statement: My body is a sacrifice to You.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Therefore I urge you, brothers, on the basis of God's mercy, to offer your bodies as living sacrifices, holy and pleasing to God, which is your spiritual service of worship.

release.Prompt Statement: I offer my mind and thoughts.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Finally, brothers, whatever is true, whatever is honorable, whatever is right, whatever is pure, whatever is lovely, whatever is admirable — if anything is excellent or praiseworthy — think on these things.

release.Prompt Statement: Work, effort, service, energy — the strength You gift.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Whatever you do, work at it with your whole being, for the Lord and not for men.

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my will to Your desire.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "Father, if You are willing, take this cup from Me. Yet not My will, but Yours be done."

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my expectations.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): A man's heart plans his course, but the LORD determines his steps.

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my emotions to You.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Trust in Him at all times, O people; pour out your hearts before Him. God is our refuge.

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my senses. I gouge out my eye. Covenant with vision.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I have made a covenant with my eyes. How then could I gaze at a young woman?

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my worry and care.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Cast all your anxiety on Him, because He cares for you.

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my hope.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): And now, Lord, for what do I wait? My hope is in You.

release.Prompt Statement: I invite Your rule in my life, my family, my church, my nation.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Your kingdom come, Your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender my image and reputation.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): but emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant, being made in human likeness.

release.Prompt Statement: I surrender to Your control and trust You will control it.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways acknowledge Him, and He will make your paths straight.

release.Prompt Statement: I invite You, Holy Spirit, to speak to my mind and control my mind.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The mind of the flesh is death, but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace.

release.Prompt Statement: Don't answer my list — give me Your list.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): "For My thoughts are not your thoughts, neither are your ways My ways," declares the LORD. "For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so My ways are higher than your ways and My thoughts than your thoughts."

release.Prompt Statement: Don't give what I want; give what You want for me.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): And we know that God works all things together for the good of those who love Him, who are called according to His purpose.

release.Prompt Statement: Your list for me is better than my list for me.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Now to Him who is able to do immeasurably more than all we ask or imagine, according to His power that is at work within us.

release.Prompt Statement: Knowing You won't fill what's full. Let me empty myself.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Let this mind be in you which was also in Christ Jesus, who, existing in the form of God, did not consider equality with God something to be grasped, but emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant...

release.Prompt Statement: Return to Him what was given by Him.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): But who am I, and who are my people, that we should be able to give as generously as this? For everything comes from You, and from Your own hand we have given to You.

release.Prompt Statement: Surrender focus that attempts to control health.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Who of you by worrying can add a single hour to his life?

release.Prompt Statement: Crush my idols. It's all You. Not my strength but Your strength in me.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): But He said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for My power is perfected in weakness." Therefore I will boast all the more gladly in my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest on me.

release.Prompt Statement: My existence is a gift returned in sacrifice.
release.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): But who am I, and who are my people, that we should be able to give as generously as this? For everything comes from You, and from Your own hand we have given to You.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Guard them.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to You. Holy Father, protect them by Your name, the name You gave Me, so that they may be one as We are one. While I was with them, I kept them and preserved them by Your name, the name You gave Me.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Fill them with joy.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): But now I am coming to You, and I speak these things while I am in the world, so that they may have My joy fulfilled within them.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Keep them safe from the evil one.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I am not asking that You take them out of the world, but that You keep them from the evil one.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Make them holy by Your truth. Your word is truth.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Sanctify them by the truth; Your word is truth.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Teach them Your word.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Sanctify them by the truth; Your word is truth.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Open the eyes of their heart.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I pray that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened, so that you may know the hope of His calling, the riches of His glorious inheritance in the saints.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Thank God for their faith.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): We always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you, because we have heard of your faith in Christ Jesus and of the love you have for all the saints.

ask.Prayer Prompt: That they would know Him and His will.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For this reason, since the day we heard about you, we have not stopped praying for you and asking God to fill you with the knowledge of His will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Wisdom and understanding.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For this reason, since the day we heard about you, we have not stopped praying for you and asking God to fill you with the knowledge of His will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding.

ask.Prayer Prompt: To live honorable and pleasing lives.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): so that you may walk in a manner worthy of the Lord and may please Him in every way: bearing fruit in every good work, growing in the knowledge of God.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Strength to endure and persist.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): being strengthened with all power according to His glorious might so that you may have full endurance and patience,

ask.Prayer Prompt: Joy and gratitude.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): being strengthened with all power according to His glorious might so that you may have full endurance and patience, and joyfully giving thanks to the Father, who has qualified you to share in the inheritance of the saints in the light.

ask.Prayer Prompt: That God would be glorified.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): After Jesus had spoken these things, He lifted up His eyes to heaven and said, "Father, the hour has come. Glorify Your Son, that Your Son may glorify You. For You granted Him authority over all people, so that He may give eternal life to all those You have given Him."

ask.Prayer Prompt: That they would know God — the only true God.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Now this is eternal life, that they may know You, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom You have sent.

ask.Prayer Prompt: That they would keep Your word.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I have revealed Your name to those You gave Me out of the world. They were Yours; You gave them to Me, and they have kept Your word.

ask.Prayer Prompt: That they would know everything is a gift.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Now they know that everything You have given Me comes from You.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Spreading the message. That they would accept and believe.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For I have given them the words You gave Me, and they have received them. They knew with certainty that I came from You, and they believed that You sent Me.

ask.Prayer Prompt: His glory in us.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): All I have is Yours, and all You have is Mine; and I have been glorified in them.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Unity — that they may be one.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I am no longer in the world, but they are in the world, and I am coming to You. Holy Father, protect them by Your name, the name You gave Me, so that they may be one as We are one.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Separation from the world — set apart.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I have given them Your word, and the world has hated them; for they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world. I am not asking that You take them out of the world, but that You keep them from the evil one. They are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Roots — that they would abide in You. Fruits — that others would believe.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): that all of them may be one, as You, Father, are in Me, and I am in You. May they also be in Us, so that the world may believe that You sent Me.

ask.Prayer Prompt: That they would understand the glory You have gifted.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I have given them the glory You gave Me, so that they may be one as We are one.

ask.Prayer Prompt: That Christ would dwell in them. That the world sees God's love and unity.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): I in them and You in Me — that they may be perfectly united, so that the world may know that You sent Me and have loved them just as You have loved Me.

ask.Prayer Prompt: To be with Christ and see the fullness of His glory.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Father, I want those You have given Me to be with Me where I am, that they may see the glory You gave Me because You loved Me before the foundation of the world.

ask.Prayer Prompt: Be revealed among us and to the world. Fill us with Your love.
ask.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Righteous Father, although the world has not known You, I know You, and these know that You sent Me. I have made Your name known to them and will continue to make it known, so that the love You have for Me may be in them, and I Myself may be in them.

yearn.Confession Prompt: Search me for the lust of the eyes.
yearn.Guidance: What am I looking at that I should not? Where have my eyes led my heart?
yearn.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For all that is in the world — the desires of the flesh, the desires of the eyes, and the pride of life — is not from the Father but from the world.

yearn.Guidance: Where have I indulged the flesh instead of walking by the Spirit?
yearn.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.

yearn.Confession Prompt: Search me for the pride of life and accomplishments.
yearn.Guidance: Where have I taken credit for what You gave? Where is my identity built on performance?
yearn.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall.

yearn.Guidance: Where have I demanded my timeline over Yours?
yearn.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): My beloved brothers, understand this: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to anger, for man's anger does not bring about the righteousness that God desires.

yearn.Guidance: Where have I let minor offenses produce major reactions?
yearn.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): A man's insight gives him patience, and his virtue is to overlook an offense.

yearn.Confession Prompt: Expose the sin hidden by my own deceit.
yearn.Guidance: Let me see what I have been hiding from myself. My heart is deceitful; only You can search it.
yearn.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): The heart is deceitful above all things and desperately wicked. Who can understand it? I, the LORD, search the heart; I examine the mind to reward a man according to his way, by what his deeds deserve.

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: Remember all I have been forgiven. We all have heart cancer.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Be kind and tenderhearted to one another, forgiving each other just as in Christ God forgave you.

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: I am one of the 'difficult' people.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: Base coaches, not umpires. Don't swing at every pitch.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Who are you to judge someone else's servant? To his own master he stands or falls. And he will stand, for the Lord is able to make him stand.

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: No one owes me an apology, respect, or attention. Because I am a debtor.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Then the master summoned him and said, 'You wicked servant! I forgave all that debt of yours because you begged me. Shouldn't you have had mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had on you?'

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: God will judge. I have no image to protect and no reputation to uphold.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Do not avenge yourselves, beloved, but leave room for God's wrath. For it is written: "Vengeance is Mine; I will repay, says the Lord."

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: I owe more than I am owed.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): As he began the settlement, a man who owed him ten thousand talents was brought to him. Since the man was unable to pay, the master ordered that he and his wife and children and all his possessions be sold to pay the debt. Then the servant fell on his knees before him. 'Be patient with me,' he begged, 'and I will pay back everything.' The master of that servant had compassion, released him, and forgave his debt.

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: God loves hateful, mean people. He loves my enemies. I must be like Him.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you, that you may be sons of your Father in heaven. For He causes His sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.

invite.Forgiveness Prompt: All infected. All have rags. Make allowance for each other's faults.
invite.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Bear with one another and forgive any complaint you may have against someone else. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.
But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.

navigate.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

navigate.Resistance Prompt: Help me realize I have no obligation to obey temptation.
navigate.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): For sin shall not be your master, because you are not under law, but under grace.

navigate.Resistance Prompt: This is a test of my trust in You.
navigate.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): No temptation has seized you except what is common to man. And God is faithful; He will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, He will also provide an escape, so that you can stand up under it.

navigate.Resistance Prompt: I yield my senses. I gouge out my eye. Covenant with my vision.
navigate.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): If your right eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.

navigate.Resistance Prompt: Let me offer unqualified availability to Your purposes, not my desires.
navigate.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Do not present the parts of your body to sin as instruments of wickedness, but present yourselves to God as those who have been brought from death to life; and present the parts of your body to Him as instruments of righteousness.

guard.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): Be sober-minded and alert. Your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion, seeking someone to devour.

guard.Protection Prompt: Hide my weakness behind Your strength.
guard.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): But He said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you, for My power is perfected in weakness." Therefore I will boast all the more gladly in my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest on me.

guard.Protection Prompt: Make me deaf to evil's suggestion.
guard.Primary Scripture Text (BSB): In addition to all this, take up the shield of faith, with which you can extinguish all the flaming arrows of the evil one.
